channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How a Minnesota limo driver invented the tech that’s changing college football
item.description: College football is suddenly in a constant state of change. The transfer portal, conference realignment, players making millions in NIL deals, the first-ever 12-team playoff—if you cryogenically froze someone five years ago and woke them up today, they wouldn’t recognize the sport.
item.dc:creator: Paul Mueller, Fast Company

item.title: Meloni says Italy is exploring deals on telecoms security, but denies private talks with Musk
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy’s Premier Giorgia Meloni said Thursday that her government is in talks with several private companies, including Elon Musk’s SpaceX, over the country’s telecoms security system, but denied having discussed the issue privately with Musk. “I never talked about this with Musk.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Los CDO pasan a ser el centro de atención de las estrategias empresariales
item.description: Dado que los datos son fundamentales para todos los aspectos de la empresa, el director de Datos se ha convertido en un ejecutivo altamente estratégico. El CDO de hoy se centra en ayudar a la organización a aprovechar los datos como un activo empresarial para impulsar los resultados.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Los CIO están replanteándose cómo utilizan los servicios de nube pública
item.description: En los últimos años, las empresas se han esforzado por trasladar la mayor cantidad posible de recursos lo más rápido posible a la nube pública para minimizar los gastos de capital y ahorrar dinero. Sin embargo, cada vez más, los CIO están revisando y racionalizando dichas inversiones.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Meta tries letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings as way to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Meta Tests eBay Listings on Facebook Marketplace in EU and US
item.description: Meta tests feature allowing users to browse eBay listings on Facebook Marketplace, resolving anticompetitive charges by the European Union.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Meta rolls back hate speech rules as Zuckerberg cites 'recent elections' as a catalyst
item.description: It wasn't just fact-checking that Meta scrapped from its platforms as it prepares for the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Japan links this Chinese hacking group to cyberattacks on its national security
item.description: Japan on Wednesday linked more than 200 cyberattacks over the past five years targeting the country’s national security and high technology data to a Chinese hacking group, MirrorFace, detailing their tactics and calling on government agencies and businesses to reinforce preventive measures.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: From AI assistants to holographic displays, automakers showcase in-cabin experiences at CES
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Interior lighting aimed at reducing motion sickness. Shiny holographic dashboards displayed on windshields. And AI-powered voice assistants paired with infotainment systems to keep you company on long drives.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Federal News Network’s AI & Data Exchange 2025
item.description: AI in action: How agencies are building out trusted artificial intelligence capabilities The post Federal News Network’s AI & Data Exchange 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Federal cyber leaders on delivering innovation
item.description: Need innovation inspiration when it comes to cyber? The post Federal cyber leaders on delivering innovation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: In the social media wars, Bluesky is destroying Truth Social
item.description: In November 2024, a left-leaning cohort of social users ran to Bluesky. The movement made the platform a liberal haven—and more popular than far-right alternative Truth Social. According to a new report from The Righting, Bluesky’s November user share toppled Truth Social’s.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: New research shows a quarter of freshwater animals are threatened with extinction
item.description: New research shows about a quarter of animals living in rivers, lakes and other freshwater sources are threatened with extinction.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Oracle offers price-performance boost with Exadata X11M update
item.description: The new version of Oracle’s Exadata platform, X11M, is now generally available, and promises improved performance on generative AI tasks such as retrieval augmented generation (RAG) for the same price as the old version.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Japan links Chinese hacker MirrorFace to dozens of cyberattacks targeting security and tech data
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan on Wednesday linked more than 200 cyberattacks over the past five years targeting the country's national security and high technology data to a Chinese hacking group, MirrorFace, detailing their tactics and calling on government agencies and businesses to reinforce preventive meas
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Uber is replacing Lyft in a points partnership with Delta
item.description: Lyft and Delta are ending their points-earning loyalty partnership in April and the airline will start a new program with rideshare competitor Uber. Uber announced the multiyear strategic partnership on Tuesday evening at the CES tech conference in Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Point2 aims to cut data center power consumption through smart cabling
item.description: AI applications and ML workloads account for nearly 20% of data center electricity consumption today, according to the Electric Power Research Institute (EPRI), and data centers are projected to represent up to 9.1% of total U.S. electricity generation annually by 2030.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Gleam 1.7 brings faster record updates
item.description: Gleam 1.7 has arrived with faster record updates and a dynamic coder for a custom type. Overall, the latest version of Gleam touts performance and package publishing improvements. This latest update to Gleam was announced January 5 and is accessible from GitHub.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: White House new cyber EO would give CISA more authorities
item.description: The White House is prepping a new cybersecurity executive order that would address cloud security, give CISA new authorities to do threat hunting and more. The post White House new cyber EO would give CISA more authorities first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: Editor’s note: This is an archive of 2024 incidents as tracked by Cisco subsidiary ThousandEyes. For current trends, see the 2025 outage report and internet health check, which is updated weekly.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: With o3 having reached AGI, OpenAI turns its sights toward superintelligence
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has reinvigorated discussion of artificial general intelligence (AGI), boldly claiming that his company’s newest model has reached that milestone.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft will invest $80B in AI data centers in fiscal 2025
item.description: Microsoft is on track to invest around $80 billion this fiscal year to build out AI-enabled data centers, company president Brad Smith said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fubo combining with Disney's Hulu + Live TV; lawsuit against Venu Sports settled
item.description: Disney's Hulu + Live TV and sports streaming service Fubo are combining in a deal that will also see the settlement of a lawsuit against the creation of Venu Sports.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AI revolution drives demand for specialized chips, reshaping global markets
item.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has rapidly transformed the chip industry since its mainstream arrival over the past two years, driving demand for specialized processors, accelerating design innovation, and reshaping global supply chains and markets.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cyber security agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FAA cancels public meeting on SpaceX due to Carter Day of Mourning
item.description: A day of public meetings scheduled in South Texas on SpaceX's Starship launches has been canceled due to National Day of Mourning for former President Jimmy Carter's death.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Malicious apps posing as VPNs can turn your device into a tool for cyberattacks
item.description: Not all apps out there offer legit virtual private networks, according to Kurt the CyberGuy. He explains how to keep your information safe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Create custom visuals on your iPhone with Image Playground in iOS 18.2
item.description: Apple's Image Playground transforms text prompts into images or animations in seconds, brining a world of creativity to the palm of your hand.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The step I take to clear my inbox every January
item.description: It's easy to find yourself swamped in unread emails. Here are a few simple steps to archiving them and clearing out some of your digital clutter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: No surprise: AI, cyber continued to dominate in 2024
item.description: Current and former federal technology executives weighed in on what topics drove the storylines in 2024. The post No surprise: AI, cyber continued to dominate in 2024 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network
